curation.synopsis: A girl stumbles half-dead across the moors and tells the strangers who shelter her of Calignius — a smog-shrouded mining town high in the Blood-Rock Peaks, gripped by a strange epidemic and the slow, watchful sway of a forlorn-looking statue at its centre. Runar Skoldolfr, a hunter, and Tiberius Grim, a veteran priest of Sigmar, lead a small expedition into the haunted vales to answer her plea. What they find is a settlement ruled by old fears and louder fanaticism — friendships souring, faith boiling into anguish, long-buried nightmares stirring under the streets. Separated and isolated, hunter and priest learn there are far worse fates than death, and must find a way to stand together against the daemons that have set themselves between them.

flags.note: Roster places this in the W40K domain, but Black-Eyed Saint is an Age of Sigmar novel and the third entry in Dale Lucas' Tales of Mhurghast series
